item.title: The scoop on the largest restructuring in history
item.description: Venezuela is set to reveal a $240bn debt pile, much higher than previously thought

item.title: Struggling European carmakers have options, none of them good
item.description: Forging alliances with China would help the struggling sector offload some of its bloated costs. It may be the pick of a bad bunch

item.title: Qatar says US-Iran hotline essential to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed says Gulf state will resume normal LNG production within weeks

item.title: BT’s pension fund lost £300mn on Thames Water
item.description: One of UK’s biggest private schemes wrote off stake in beleaguered utility in 2024

item.title: The red flags at HSBC over funds linked to Lebanese central bank scandal
item.description: The lender’s Swiss unit is under formal investigation over whether it helped Riad Salame embezzle funds

item.title: How a $20bn bet paid off for Africa’s richest man
item.description: Critics dub Aliko Dangote a crony capitalist. He says he is pioneering Asian-style economic development

item.title: Private equity’s move into youth sports brings some truths home
item.description: Investments create benefits but also lead to higher costs and risk of inequality

item.title: US defence groups to meet Trump as missile production struggles
item.description: The administration is seeking to rebuild conventional weapons stockpiles while regearing for modern warfare

item.title: The US dollar’s ‘immigrant’ origins
item.description: The story of the hapless count and the Bohemian silver mine that created the dollar.

item.title: Coming soon: Rewiring sport
item.description: Is technology fundamentally changing the nature of sport?

item.title: Venezuela faces world’s largest debt restructuring
item.description: Caracas will reveal $240bn debt pile as it seeks re-entry into global markets

item.title: Nvidia’s banned AI chips double in price on China’s black market
item.description: US crackdown on illicit exports has made it riskier, harder and more expensive to buy tech giant’s processors

item.title: British households not electrifying fast enough, says government climate adviser
item.description: Report argues that switching to heat pumps and electric cars would bring down energy bills and avoid price shocks

item.title: Hormuz closure strands almost 1,200 cargo ships with $125bn worth of goods
item.description: ‘Unprecedented’ blockade of the strait raises concerns about the future of global maritime trade, says insurer Allianz

item.title: Germany set to scrap plans to build its biggest warship since second world war
item.description: Berlin wants to ditch F126 frigate programme after cost overruns and delays

item.title: EU allies join US pact to break reliance on Chinese AI supply chains
item.description: Jacob Helberg, architect of Pax Silica, tells the FT the American-led group will boost innovation

item.title: Ari Emanuel in talks to buy West End and Broadway giant ATG for $6bn
item.description: People familiar with the situation hope deal can be concluded soon with theatre group’s private equity owner Providence

item.title: FirstFT: Alibaba sues US defence department over inclusion on Chinese military blacklist
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, Takaichi shuns Japan’s business elites, and China’s biggest IPO in four years

item.title: US chipmakers lead Wall Street slide on rate rise worries
item.description: Nasdaq declines for second day with companies such as Nvidia under pressure

item.title: How to surf turbulent markets
item.description: Is the wild ride worth it?

item.title: Acclaimed bankruptcy lawyer James Sprayregen set to join Paul Weiss
item.description: Former Kirkland & Ellis partner helped turn firm into a restructuring powerhouse

item.title: Alibaba sues Pentagon over inclusion on Chinese military blacklist
item.description: Ecommerce giant claims US defence department’s ‘arbitrary and capricious’ decision lacked evidence

item.title: EG Group files plan for US listing that could raise $1bn
item.description: IPO could value UK-based petrol station empire at more than $9bn

item.title: SpaceX pitches investors juicy yields in $25bn bond deal
item.description: Bankers upsize offering after high borrowing costs lure investors

item.title: UK tax loophole used by Shein will not be closed until October 2028
item.description: Retailers say slow pace of action undermines efforts to rejuvenate high streets

item.title: Eli Lilly and Bayer hit out at Germany’s spending on new medicines
item.description: Pharma groups indicate support for US pushback on legislation to lower drug prices

item.title: US Supreme Court limits scope of foreign human rights claims
item.description: Justices refuse to consider complaint alleging Cisco enabled Chinese surveillance of banned religious group

item.title: SpaceX tests its reality-warping powers on the bond market
item.description: For some buyers, a solid rating can act as permission to buy; the lack of one can put a security off limits

item.title: AI’s money machine faces first real test in Manhattan primary
item.description: Industry spends millions to target candidate who supports greater regulation of the technology

item.title: Drone start-up Stark wins more funding from Peter Thiel-backed firm
item.description: US billionaire’s fund participated in €500mn funding round despite previous criticism of company’s links with financier
